summary: Today’s PaymentsDive recap: 8 new stories, with focus on regulation, product + fintech innovation.

articles.title: Payments M&A spools up
articles.description: Stripe’s potential bid for PayPal isn’t the only merger and acquisition activity underway as deal-making revs up for the second half of the year.

articles.title: Why pricey credit cards may soon cost more
articles.description: A settlement over swipe fees could mark a major shift for retail payments as merchants embrace surcharges on high-end cards.

articles.title: The next competitive edge is the payout experience
articles.description: Spending is instant. Getting paid back is not. Learn why that payout gap will impact your business.

articles.title: Retail media was the first wave. Financial services may define the next one.
articles.description: Retail media changed advertising. Financial services could define what comes next.

articles.title: Visa, Stripe join AI protocol project
articles.description: A pack of major fintechs have joined a nonprofit seeking to standardize how payments are sent online using artificial intelligence and software.

articles.title: Backing the president’s fintech order
articles.description: “Regulation should preserve confidence in the system, but it should not inhibit responsible innovation,” writes one financial services lawyer.

articles.title: EWA backers push for CFPB move
articles.description: Supporters of earned wage access aim to update national oversight this year, backing a new law and seeking to confirm a Trump policy change.

articles.title: Bottomline adds stablecoin tools
articles.description: The company is betting the new digital currency will gain traction across corporate payment, treasury and liquidity workflows.
